para_9: You think about today, not tomorrow. Your paycheck is already spent. You don't plan months or years ahead. Retirement feels far away. This short-term focus is common in the US, where instant gratification is built into everything. Same-day delivery. Binge-watching. Fast food. But not planning ahead means you're always reacting to problems. When something goes wrong, you have no cushion. You scramble. Put it on credit. The cycle repeats. This keeps you living paycheck to paycheck, one emergency from disaster. Short-term thinking also means missing long-term opportunities. You don't invest because you need money now. But future you is going to need money too. Your brain is wired to prioritize immediate needs. Breaking this pattern means forcing long-term thinking. Automate savings. Set up retirement contributions. Build an emergency fund, even $10 at a time. Make future you a priority.
